title: Epidendrum duboisianum
taxonomy: Epidendrum duboisianum is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This species is further categorized as a member of the subfamily Epidendroideae. More specifically, it is placed within the tribe Maxillarieae. Within the tribe, it is classified as a member of the subtribe Cymbidiinae. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other orchids.

intro: Epidendrum duboisianum, commonly known as the 'Doris' orchid, is a type of epiphytic orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, pain, and respiratory issues, due to its purported antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Epidendrum duboisianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including colds, bronchitis, and skin conditions.
